text.title: React Project Structure and Component Rules
text.summary: Feature-based folder layout and typed functional component conventions for maintainable React apps.

rule.statement: Organize code by feature, not by file type. Each feature owns its components, hooks, API client, and types.
rule.rationale: Feature cohesion reduces cross-directory jumps during changes; file-type grouping (components/, hooks/, types/) creates scattered coupling.

example.code: src/ ├── components/ # Shared UI primitives (Button, Input) │ └── ui/ ├── features/ │ ├── auth/ │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── hooks/ │ │ ├── api.ts │ │ └── types.ts │ └── dashboard/ ├── hooks/ # Truly shared custom hooks ├── utils/ # Pure utility functions ├── types/ # Shared TypeScript types ├── lib/ # Third-party wrappers └── app/ # Next.js app directory / routes

rule.statement: Use function declarations (not arrow functions) for components; always declare props as a named interface.
rule.rationale: Function declarations are hoisted and show the component name in stack traces; named prop interfaces document the API and enable IDE autocomplete.

antipattern.description: Exporting anonymous arrow function components: export default () => <div />
antipattern.reason: Anonymous components show as "Unknown" in React DevTools and error stack traces, making debugging harder.
